mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 4.33% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained elevation of FEDFUNDS above 4.0% signals continued monetary tightening, which compresses near-term equity valuations and increases discount rates, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors demand higher compensation for duration risk.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS remaining above 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.2% combined with payroll employment at 159,561 thousand indicates a tight labor market approaching full employment. Persistent low unemployment signals wage pressure and inflation risk, forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase rates, which drives 10-year Treasury yields upward as inflation expectations embed into long-duration yields.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.5% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% and PAYEMS at 159,561k as of t-1mo
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls over 60 trading days despite UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS growth positive, or correlation between (UNRATE, PAYEMS) and yield direction is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 724,131 million and housing starts (HOUST) at 1,361 thousand demonstrate robust aggregate demand. Strong demand growth signals inflation acceleration and reduces the probability of near-term rate cuts, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as the market reprices terminal rate expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% OR HOUST > 1,200 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 724,131M and HOUST at 1,361k as of t-2mo
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20 trading days when both RSAFS and HOUST signals are active, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 103.88 reflects supply-side capacity utilization. Sustained INDPRO growth above 103.5 signals tight supply conditions and inflationary pressure, prompting expectations of higher terminal rates and causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as inflation risk premiums increase.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls over 60 trading days despite INDPRO > 103.5, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 24-month rolling window in supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 320.58 is elevated relative to historical norms. If CPI continues to rise or remains sticky above current levels, the Federal Reserve will maintain restrictive policy, preventing rate cuts and pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as long-term inflation expectations remain anchored above pre-crisis levels.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20 trading days when CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.2%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 23,528 billion (chained 2017 dollars) reflects accumulated economic growth. Sustained GDP growth above trend signals demand-driven inflation and reduces recession risk, causing investors to demand higher yields on 10-year Treasuries as the probability of rate cuts diminishes and terminal rate expectations rise.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls over 60 trading days despite positive GDP growth trajectory, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 36-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 159,561 thousand combined with unemployment at 4.2% creates a tight labor market. A joint signal of rising PAYEMS and stable low UNRATE indicates wage inflation risk, forcing the Fed to maintain higher rates longer, which pushes 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market prices in persistent monetary restriction.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159,561k and UNRATE at 4.2% as of t-1mo
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage pressure
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20 trading days when both PAYEMS and UNRATE trigger conditions are met, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1,361 thousand signal strong residential investment demand. Sustained HOUST above 1,300 thousand indicates robust construction activity and household formation, which increases aggregate demand and inflation expectations, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the Fed maintains restrictive policy to combat demand-driven price pressures.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 5 trading days when HOUST > 1,300 thousand, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 12-month rolling window in housing-led regimes.
